original_hypothesis.title: recover() Missing Whitelist Check Allows Owner to Mint Unbacked asdUSDC
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: ASDUSDC.recover() does not check whitelistedUSDCVersions before minting asdUSDC. An owner (or compromised owner key) can call recover() with any ERC20 token address, minting asdUSDC backed by worthless tokens. The owner then withdraws legitimate USDC via withdraw(), draining depositors' reserves. Even accidentally, recovering a non-USDC token inflates asdUSDC supply without proper backing.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Both deposit() and withdraw() check the whitelist, but recover() does not. This asymmetry allows the owner to bypass the intended token quality gate, breaking the conservation invariant between asdUSDC supply and USDC reserves.

proof_of_signal.pattern: recover() has no require(whitelistedUSDCVersions[_usdcVersion]) check; mints asdUSDC for any token balance difference

preconditions: Some worthless ERC20 token has been sent to the ASDUSDC contract (or owner sends one)
Legitimate USDC deposits exist in the contract

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: amountToRecover = ERC20(_usdcVersion).balanceOf(address(this)) - usdcBalances[_usdcVersion]; for any non-tracked token, usdcBalances is 0, so full balance is 'recoverable'
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Owner sends 1M worthless tokens → calls recover(worthlessToken) → gets 1M * 10^(18-decimals) asdUSDC → withdraws legitimate USDC
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Owner extracts all deposited USDC by diluting asdUSDC supply with worthless backing

economic_questions: Is the owner a multisig with timelock?
What is the total value of USDC deposited in the contract?
Could this be triggered accidentally with airdropped tokens?
validation_requirements: Test recover() with non-whitelisted token address
Verify minted asdUSDC can be used to withdraw legitimate USDC
Confirm no whitelist check exists in recover()

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Cost of sending worthless tokens to contract (near zero)
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Up to 100% of all deposited USDC

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Up to 100% loss of deposited USDC

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Check owner address type (EOA vs multisig)
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Trace recover() → withdraw() flow with non-whitelisted token

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
